proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Stage IV disease (stage_iv=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than non-stage IV.
Patients with brain metastases (has_brain_mets=1) have shorter pfs_months than those without.
Older age (age_years) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Female sex (sex_female=1) is associated with longer pfs_months than male sex.
Node-positive disease (node_positive=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than node-negative.
Postmenopausal status (postmenopausal=1) is associated with longer pfs_months than premenopausal.

analyses.result_summary: ECOG 0/1/2 mean PFS = 5.64 / 4.44 / 3.29 months; ANOVA F=2860.7, p<1e-300. Strongly significant; higher ECOG → much shorter PFS (sign of effect from ECOG2-ECOG0 = -2.35).
Stage IV (n=14969) mean=3.62 vs non-stage-IV (n=35031) mean=5.15; diff=-1.54 mo, t=-66.6, p≈0.
Brain mets (n=5090) mean=3.75 vs none (n=44910) mean=4.80; diff=-1.05 mo, p=1.5e-178.

Counterintuitively, age is STRONGLY POSITIVELY correlated with PFS: Pearson r=+0.70, p≈0. Direction opposite to hypothesis. Older patients have markedly longer PFS in this cohort — likely reflects selection (older ER+ HR+ patients receiving CDK4/6 inhibitor / endocrine therapy and excluding the most aggressive young-onset disease).

Female mean PFS 4.69 (n=22371) vs male mean 4.70 (n=27629); diff=-0.013, p=0.56. Not significant. (Note: cohort is breast cancer; ~45% are coded male, suggesting male breast cancer / coding artifact.)
Node+ mean 4.68 vs node- mean 4.70; diff=-0.024, p=0.29. Not significant.
Postmeno mean 4.68 vs premeno mean 4.71; diff=-0.023, p=0.31. Not significant on its own.

proposed_hypotheses.text: ER-positive patients (er_positive=1) have longer pfs_months than ER-negative patients.
PR-positive patients (pr_positive=1) have longer pfs_months than PR-negative patients.
HER2-positive patients (her2_positive=1) have shorter pfs_months than HER2-negative patients (in the absence of effective HER2-targeted therapy).
HER2-low patients (her2_low=1) have different pfs_months than HER2-non-low patients.
BRCA1 mutation carriers have shorter pfs_months than BRCA1 wild-type.
BRCA2 mutation carriers have shorter pfs_months than BRCA2 wild-type.
PIK3CA mutation carriers have shorter pfs_months than PIK3CA wild-type.
Higher Ki-67 percentage (ki67_pct) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Larger tumor size (tumor_size_cm) is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: ER+ (n=34886) mean PFS=4.86 vs ER- (n=15114) mean=4.30; diff=+0.57, t=25.6, p=3.7e-143. Strongly significant in expected direction.
PR+ mean=4.82 vs PR- mean=4.46; diff=+0.36, p=5.8e-59.
HER2+ mean=4.32 vs HER2- mean=4.78; diff=-0.46, p=6.1e-70.
HER2-low mean=4.76 vs HER2-non-low mean=4.65; diff=+0.11, p=1.0e-6. Small but significant positive.
BRCA1+ mean=4.67 vs WT mean=4.69; diff=-0.024, p=0.72. Not significant.
BRCA2+ mean=4.57 vs WT mean=4.70; diff=-0.13, p=0.08. Borderline; trend toward shorter PFS.
PIK3CA+ (n=17365) mean=4.33 vs PIK3CA- (n=32635) mean=4.89; diff=-0.56, p=6.1e-144. Strongly negative — PIK3CA mutation is a robust adverse prognostic marker.
Pearson r(ki67_pct, pfs_months) = -0.091, p=3e-93. Significant negative correlation as expected (proliferation marker).
Pearson r(tumor_size_cm, pfs_months) = +0.0004, p=0.93. Null. Surprising — but consistent with tumor size being a poor solo prognostic marker once stage and biology are considered.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher serum albumin (albumin_g_dl) is associated with longer pfs_months (better nutrition / less inflammation).
Higher LDH (ldh_u_l) is associated with shorter pfs_months (tumor burden / hemolysis surrogate).
Higher CRP (crp_mg_l) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio (nlr) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Greater 6-month weight loss percentage (weight_loss_pct_6mo) is associated with shorter pfs_months (cachexia).

Other routine labs (hemoglobin, alkaline phosphatase, AST, ALT, total bilirubin, creatinine, BUN, sodium, potassium, calcium) have weak or no main-effect associations with pfs_months once major factors are accounted for.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r(albumin_g_dl, pfs_months) = +0.100, p=9e-112. Significant positive — higher albumin → longer PFS.
r(ldh, pfs) = -0.016, p=2e-4. Statistically significant in expected direction but tiny effect size.
r(crp, pfs) = -0.003, p=0.46. Null.
r(nlr, pfs) = +0.003, p=0.54. Null.
r(weight_loss_pct_6mo, pfs) = -0.116, p=4e-148. Significant: more weight loss → shorter PFS, as expected for cachexia.

r values for hemoglobin, alk phos, AST, ALT, total bilirubin, creatinine, BUN, Na, K, Ca with pfs_months are all |r|<=0.014. None practically meaningful; only ALK-phos (p=0.003), bilirubin (p=0.046), K (p=0.025), Ca (p=0.004) reach nominal significance because of n=50000.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Tamoxifen treatment (treatment_tamoxifen=1) is associated with longer pfs_months than no tamoxifen (overall, unselected).
Palbociclib (treatment_palbociclib=1) is associated with longer pfs_months than no palbociclib (overall).
Trastuzumab (treatment_trastuzumab=1) is associated with longer pfs_months than no trastuzumab (overall).
Olaparib (treatment_olaparib=1) is associated with longer pfs_months than no olaparib (overall).
Sacituzumab govitecan (treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan=1) is associated with longer pfs_months than no sacituzumab (overall).
Pembrolizumab (treatment_pembrolizumab=1) is associated with longer pfs_months than no pembrolizumab (overall).

analyses.result_summary: Tamoxifen mean=4.72 vs none=4.68; diff=+0.043, p=0.077. Not significant.
Palbociclib mean=5.41 vs none=4.31; diff=+1.10, t=43.5, p≈0. STRONG positive overall effect — but unadjusted; needs subgroup analysis since palbociclib indication is restricted to ER+/HER2- disease.
Trastuzumab mean=4.64 vs none=4.71; diff=-0.060, p=0.029. Marginally negative — but unadjusted; trastuzumab is given to HER2+ (worse-prognosis) patients.
Olaparib mean=4.66 vs none=4.70; diff=-0.036, p=0.33. Not significant overall.
Sacituzumab mean=4.69 vs none=4.69; diff=-0.007, p=0.85. Null.
Pembrolizumab mean=4.63 vs none=4.70; diff=-0.074, p=0.018. Marginally negative overall.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Tamoxifen produces longer pfs_months in ER-positive patients (er_positive=1) than in ER-negative patients (treatment×er interaction).
Tamoxifen produces longer pfs_months in postmenopausal patients than in premenopausal patients.
Tamoxifen produces longer pfs_months in PR-positive patients than in PR-negative patients.

analyses.result_summary: Within ER+ (n=34886): tamoxifen diff=+0.014, p=0.76. Within ER- (n=15114): diff=-0.046, p=0.43. Treatment×ER interaction coef=+0.060, p=0.45. No subgroup-specific tamoxifen benefit.
Within postmeno: tamoxifen diff=+0.018, p=0.51. Within premeno: diff=+0.090, p=0.057. Interaction p=0.16. No clear postmeno-specific benefit.
Within PR+: tamoxifen diff=+0.031, p=0.35. Within PR-: diff=+0.076, p=0.064. Interaction p=0.41. No.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Palbociclib produces longer pfs_months in ER-positive patients than in ER-negative patients (palbociclib×ER interaction is positive).
Palbociclib's PFS benefit is reduced or abolished in HER2-positive patients (palbociclib×HER2 interaction is negative).
Palbociclib's PFS benefit is reduced or abolished in PIK3CA-mutant patients (palbociclib×PIK3CA interaction is negative).
Palbociclib produces longer pfs_months in postmenopausal patients than in premenopausal patients.

analyses.result_summary: Within ER+: palbo diff=+1.58 (n_tx=12194), p≈0. Within ER-: diff=-0.017 (n_tx=5234), p=0.63. Interaction coef=+1.59, p=9e-229. STRONG: palbo benefit is concentrated in ER+.
Within HER2+: palbo diff=-0.014, p=0.77. Within HER2-: diff=+1.36, p≈0. Interaction coef=-1.36, p=2e-118. STRONG: HER2+ abolishes palbo benefit.
Within PIK3CA+: palbo diff=+0.036, p=0.29. Within PIK3CA-: diff=+1.66, p≈0. Interaction coef=-1.62, p=4e-255. STRONGEST: PIK3CA mutation abolishes palbo benefit.

Within postmeno: palbo diff=+1.04, p≈0. Within premeno: diff=+1.18, p≈0. Interaction coef=-0.13, p=0.004. The benefit exists in BOTH groups; postmeno has slightly smaller effect — opposite to mechanistic expectation but very modest.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Trastuzumab produces longer pfs_months in HER2-positive patients than in HER2-negative patients.
Trastuzumab produces longer pfs_months in HER2-low patients than in HER2-non-low patients.

analyses.result_summary: Within HER2+ (n=9060): trastuzumab diff=-0.079, p=0.15. Within HER2- (n=40940): diff=-0.051, p=0.10. Interaction coef=-0.028, p=0.66. NO benefit anywhere — including HER2+. Anomalous given external clinical evidence; trastuzumab appears inert in this dataset.
Within HER2-low: trastuzumab diff=-0.016, p=0.73. Within HER2-non-low: diff=-0.075, p=0.012. Interaction p=0.30. No HER2-low-specific benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Olaparib produces longer pfs_months in BRCA1-mutated patients than in BRCA1 wild-type.
Olaparib produces longer pfs_months in BRCA2-mutated patients than in BRCA2 wild-type.
Olaparib produces longer pfs_months in BRCA1-or-BRCA2-mutated (brca_any=1) patients than in BRCA wild-type patients (combined gBRCA modifier).

analyses.result_summary: Within BRCA1+ (n=1277): olaparib diff=+0.40 (n_tx=120), p=0.117. Trend toward benefit, n-limited.
Within BRCA2+ (n=1222): olaparib diff=+0.30 (n_tx=119), p=0.19. Trend.
Within BRCA-any+: olaparib diff=+0.35, p=0.041 (n_tx=239). Within BRCA-WT: diff=-0.056, p=0.14 (n_tx=4707). Interaction coef=+0.40, p=0.021. Pooling BRCA1/2 reveals significant olaparib benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Sacituzumab govitecan produces longer pfs_months in triple-negative breast cancer (er_positive=0 AND pr_positive=0 AND her2_positive=0) than in non-TNBC.
Sacituzumab govitecan produces longer pfs_months in HER2-low patients than in HER2-non-low patients.

analyses.result_summary: Within TNBC: sacit diff=-0.085, p=0.22. Within non-TNBC: diff=+0.019, p=0.66. Interaction coef=-0.10, p=0.25. No TNBC benefit.
Within HER2-low: sacit diff=-0.031, p=0.64. Within HER2-non-low: diff=+0.007, p=0.88. Interaction p=0.63. No.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Pembrolizumab produces longer pfs_months in TNBC patients than in non-TNBC (pembrolizumab×TNBC interaction positive).
Pembrolizumab produces longer pfs_months in high-Ki67 (≥20%) patients than in low-Ki67 patients.
Pembrolizumab produces a HARMFUL effect (shorter pfs_months) in ER+ disease relative to controls.

analyses.result_summary: Within TNBC (n=10496): pembro diff=+0.072, p=0.22. Within non-TNBC: diff=-0.114, p=0.0017. Interaction coef=+0.185, p=0.015. Qualitative interaction: pembro is mildly beneficial in TNBC (NS) and mildly harmful in non-TNBC; the difference is significant.
Within ki67-high: pembro diff=-0.071, p=0.18. Within ki67-low: diff=-0.068, p=0.069. Interaction p=0.97. No ki67 modulation.
Within ER+: pembro diff=-0.111, p=0.005. Within ER-: diff=+0.020, p=0.67. Interaction coef=-0.13, p=0.050. Weak harmful signal in ER+ disease.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable OLS model adjusting for demographics, biology, labs, and other treatments, palbociclib retains a large positive coefficient and PIK3CA, ER, HER2 remain among the dominant biology covariates.
After adjustment, ECOG performance status, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, age_years, albumin, weight_loss are independent prognostic factors for pfs_months.

analyses.code: smf.ols(pfs_months ~ all features, df) — full model R²=0.792
analyses.result_summary: Full prognostic model R²=0.792. Significant adjusted coefficients (β, p): age_years +0.176 (p≈0), ecog_ps -1.172 (p≈0), stage_iv -1.549 (p≈0), has_brain_mets -0.983 (p≈0), er_positive +0.531 (p=1e-274), her2_positive -0.471 (p=2e-239), pik3ca_mutation -0.582 (p≈0), ki67_pct -0.019 (p≈0), albumin_g_dl +0.476 (p≈0), weight_loss_pct_6mo -0.077 (p≈0), ldh_u_l -0.0005 (p=2e-16), treatment_palbociclib +1.101 (p≈0). Tamoxifen, trastuzumab, olaparib, pembrolizumab not significant after adjustment; sacituzumab very weakly +0.034 (p=0.05).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Tamoxifen produces longer pfs_months in the joint subgroup of ER+ AND postmenopausal patients than the same patients without tamoxifen.
Palbociclib produces a particularly large pfs_months benefit in the joint subgroup ER+ AND HER2- AND postmenopausal.
Trastuzumab produces longer pfs_months in HER2+ patients than the same patients without trastuzumab.
Pembrolizumab produces longer pfs_months in TNBC patients than the same patients without pembrolizumab.

analyses.result_summary: Within ER+/postmeno (n=21081): tamoxifen diff=+0.010, p=0.81. Null.
Within ER+/HER2-/postmeno (n=17289): palbo (n_tx=5987) mean=6.18 vs ctrl (n=11302) mean=4.31; diff=+1.87, p≈0. Confirms huge subgroup benefit.
Within HER2+ (n=9060): trastuzumab diff=-0.079, p=0.15. Null. Confirms no detectable trastuzumab benefit even in indication.
Within TNBC: pembro diff=+0.072, p=0.22. NS in this stratum despite significant interaction with TNBC.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across all six treatments and 19 binary candidate modifiers (incl. age≥65, sex_female, stage_iv, brain_mets, node+, postmeno, ER, PR, HER2, HER2-low, BRCA1, BRCA2, BRCA-any, PIK3CA, TNBC, ki67_high, albumin_low, ldh_high, weight_loss_5p), the strongest treatment×modifier interactions involve palbociclib with PIK3CA (negative), ER (positive), HER2 (negative), Ki67-high (negative), TNBC (negative), and PR (positive).

analyses.code: for each treatment x each modifier, fit smf.ols('pfs ~ T*M'); rank by interaction p
analyses.result_summary: Top heterogeneity: palbo×pik3ca β=-1.62 p=4e-255; palbo×er +1.59 p=9e-229; palbo×ki67_high -1.47 p=6e-169; palbo×triple_neg -1.39 p=3e-136; palbo×her2 -1.36 p=2e-118; palbo×pr +1.09 p=3e-117; palbo×her2_low +0.42 p=9e-19; pembro×triple_neg +0.19 p=0.015; sacit×albumin_low -0.21 p=0.016; olaparib×brca_any +0.40 p=0.021; tamox×stage_iv -0.12 p=0.021; sacit×ldh_high +0.17 p=0.032. Strongest by far: palbociclib's effect modulators.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous Ki-67 percentage is a quantitative modifier of palbociclib effect on pfs_months: each 1-point increase in ki67_pct reduces palbociclib's PFS gain.
Continuous albumin_g_dl is a positive modifier of sacituzumab govitecan effect on pfs_months: higher albumin amplifies sacituzumab benefit.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef = -0.052 mo per 1% Ki67 increase, p=1.5e-174. At Ki67=10% palbociclib still gains ~+1.6 mo; at Ki67=40% gain shrinks toward zero. Confirms continuous ki67 dose-response on palbo benefit (proliferation index drives non-response).
Interaction coef = +0.185 mo per 1 g/dL albumin, p=0.013. Modest dose-response: sacit benefit is more positive when albumin higher.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within ER+/HER2-/postmeno, the palbociclib PFS benefit is RESTRICTED to PIK3CA wild-type patients; PIK3CA mutation eliminates the benefit.
Within ER+/HER2-, the palbociclib PFS benefit holds in both pre- and postmenopausal patients (postmeno is not a critical effect modifier).

analyses.result_summary: Within ER+/HER2-/postmeno/PIK3CA-: palbo diff=+2.82, p≈0 (n_tx=3944). Within ER+/HER2-/postmeno/PIK3CA+: palbo diff=+0.026, p=0.65 (n_tx=2043). PIK3CA mutation completely abolishes the palbo benefit even within the otherwise responsive subgroup.
Within ER+/HER2-/postmeno: palbo diff=+1.87 (p≈0). Within ER+/HER2-/premeno: palbo diff=+2.01, p=3e-249. Premenopausal patients also benefit; postmeno is not a defining subgroup feature.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Pembrolizumab's TNBC interaction reflects qualitative reversal: pembro slightly hurts non-TNBC patients (especially ER+) but is null/slightly helpful in TNBC; no further sub-modifier within TNBC produces a significant pembro PFS benefit.

analyses.result_summary: Within TNBC sub-strata: pembro diff +0.058 (PIK3CA-, p=0.42), +0.096 (PIK3CA+, p=0.35), -0.040 (ki67-high, p=0.73), +0.112 (ki67-low, p=0.10), +0.080 (no brain mets, p=0.19), -0.061 (stage IV, p=0.56). No TNBC sub-stratum reaches conventional significance for pembrolizumab benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Olaparib's PFS benefit is concentrated in BRCA-mutated AND ER-negative patients; ER+ status attenuates olaparib benefit even in BRCA mutation carriers.

analyses.result_summary: Within BRCA-any+/ER- (n=750): olaparib diff=+0.83, p=0.0012 (n_tx=55). Within BRCA-any+/ER+ (n=1749): olaparib diff=+0.14, p=0.49 (n_tx=184). Within BRCA+/TNBC: diff=+0.75, p=0.022 (n_tx=35). 3-way interaction olaparib×brca_any×er_positive: coef=-0.67, p=0.099 (borderline). Olaparib benefit is largest and clearest in BRCA-mut + ER-negative tumors.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Trastuzumab is null/inert across every plausible subgroup (HER2+, HER2+/ER+, HER2+/ER-, HER2+/non-stage-IV, HER2+/stage-IV, HER2-low) — i.e., the dataset does not support trastuzumab efficacy as ordinarily expected.

analyses.result_summary: Trastuzumab effect by subgroup: HER2+ -0.079 (p=0.15); HER2- -0.051 (p=0.10); HER2+/ER+ -0.087 (p=0.18); HER2+/ER- -0.063 (p=0.55); HER2+/non-stage-IV -0.042 (p=0.50); HER2+/stage-IV -0.108 (p=0.24); HER2-low -0.016 (p=0.73). No subgroup shows positive PFS effect; magnitudes are uniformly small and slightly negative. Interpretation: in this synthetic / EHR cohort, trastuzumab assignment is non-informative for PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Tamoxifen does not produce a PFS benefit in ANY plausible mechanism-driven subgroup (ER+, ER+/postmeno, ER+/premeno, ER+/HER2-, ER+/HER2-/premeno, ER+/HER2-/postmeno, ER+/PR+, PR+).
Sacituzumab govitecan does not produce a PFS benefit in TNBC, HER2-low, HER2-low/ER+, HER2-low/TNBC, ER-/HER2-, or HER2- subgroups.

analyses.result_summary: All tamoxifen subgroup effects in ER+ strata: ER+ +0.023 (p=0.45), ER+/postmeno +0.010 (p=0.81), ER+/premeno +0.044 (p=0.38), ER+/HER2- +0.025 (p=0.48), ER+/HER2-/premeno +0.047 (p=0.40), ER+/HER2-/postmeno +0.009 (p=0.83), ER+/PR+ +0.031 (p=0.35), PR+ +0.036 (p=0.25). All directionally tiny, none significant. Tamoxifen appears inert.

Sacituzumab effects: TNBC -0.085 (p=0.22), HER2-low -0.031 (p=0.64), HER2-low/ER+ -0.014 (p=0.86), HER2-low/TNBC -0.072 (p=0.49), ER-/HER2- -0.088 (p=0.17), HER2- -0.028 (p=0.51). Uniformly null/slightly negative.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within ER+/HER2-/postmeno, the palbociclib PFS benefit does not depend further on stage_iv, has_brain_mets, or ECOG performance status — i.e., other prognostic factors do not modify the treatment effect after the core biology is fixed.

analyses.result_summary: Palbo within ER+/HER2-/postmeno by additional strata: non-stage-IV +1.88 (p≈0); stage-IV +1.82 (p=6e-104); no brain mets +1.86 (p≈0); ECOG 0 +1.85; ECOG 1 +1.89; ECOG 2 +1.95. The palbo benefit is essentially the same (~+1.85 mo) across stage, brain-mets, and ECOG strata. These features do NOT modulate palbo treatment effect once ER/HER2/menopausal status are fixed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with the joint phenotype ER+ AND HER2-negative AND PIK3CA-wild-type form the complete subgroup in which palbociclib produces a large PFS benefit (~+2.9 months); outside this subgroup palbociclib is not associated with longer PFS.

analyses.result_summary: Indicator subgroup defined. In subgroup (n=18681): palbo (n_tx=6571) mean=7.30 vs ctrl mean=4.40; diff=+2.91, p≈0. Out of subgroup (n=31319): palbo diff=+0.003, p=0.91. Interaction term coef=+2.91, p≈0. Triple interaction model confirms: palbo:er +2.95, palbo:er:her2 -2.89 (p=3e-81), palbo:er:pik3ca -3.01 (p=2e-165). The benefit is fully captured by the conjunction ER+ × HER2- × PIK3CA-WT.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with the joint phenotype BRCA1-or-BRCA2-mutated AND ER-negative form the subgroup with the largest detectable olaparib PFS benefit (~+0.83 months); BRCA mutation alone is sufficient for a smaller benefit (~+0.35 months) but ER+ status attenuates it.

analyses.result_summary: Olaparib in BRCA-any+/ER- (n=750): mean_tx=5.40 vs mean_ctrl=4.57; diff=+0.83, p=0.0012 (n_tx=55). Olaparib in BRCA-any+ overall: diff=+0.35, p=0.041 (n_tx=239). Olaparib×BRCA-any×ER+ interaction coef=-0.67, p=0.099 (borderline ER-attenuation). The cleanest olaparib-responsive subgroup is BRCA-mutated AND ER-negative tumors.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After multivariable adjustment for age, ECOG, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, ki67_pct, albumin, weight_loss_pct_6mo, and ldh, the palbociclib PFS effect within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA- remains large (+2.9 months) and outside the subgroup is essentially zero.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_palbociclib + age + ecog + stage_iv + brain_mets + ki67 + albumin + weight_loss + ldh', df_in_subgroup)
analyses.result_summary: Within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA- (N=18681): adjusted palbo coef = +2.92, p≈0. Outside subgroup (N=31319): adjusted palbo coef = +0.0002, p=0.91. Adjustment for prognostic covariates does NOT reduce the subgroup-specific palbo effect — confirming it is a treatment-effect modifier rather than a confounder.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within TNBC, no joint sub-phenotype (TNBC + PIK3CA-, TNBC + ki67_low, TNBC + no-brain-mets, TNBC + non-stage-IV, TNBC + BRCA-mut) produces a statistically significant pembrolizumab PFS benefit individually; the pembro×TNBC interaction reflects mainly an absence of the small harmful effect seen in non-TNBC.

analyses.result_summary: Pembro inside TNBC by sub-features: TNBC/PIK3CA- +0.058 (p=0.42), TNBC/PIK3CA+ +0.096 (p=0.35), TNBC/ki67-high -0.040 (p=0.73), TNBC/ki67-low +0.112 (p=0.10), TNBC/no-brain-mets +0.080 (p=0.19), TNBC/brain-mets -0.089 (p=0.63), TNBC/non-stage-IV +0.070 (p=0.28), TNBC/stage-IV -0.061 (p=0.56), TNBC/BRCA-mut +0.002 (p=0.99). None reach p<0.05. The TNBC interaction is driven by removal of the non-TNBC harm rather than by a discoverable benefit-defining sub-phenotype.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL palbociclib subgroup: pfs_months is longer (~+2.9 months) for palbociclib-treated patients, and only for patients meeting the joint criteria er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0; outside this subgroup the palbociclib effect on PFS is null.

FINAL olaparib subgroup: pfs_months is longer (~+0.83 months) for olaparib-treated patients meeting the joint criteria (brca1_mutation=1 OR brca2_mutation=1) AND er_positive=0; outside this subgroup the effect is small or null.

FINAL pembrolizumab heterogeneity: pfs_months trends UP (+0.07) on pembrolizumab in TNBC and DOWN (-0.11, p=0.005) in ER+ disease; the qualitative reversal across triple_neg is significant (interaction p=0.015) but no positive subgroup reaches per-stratum significance for pembrolizumab benefit.

FINAL trastuzumab/tamoxifen/sacituzumab: none of these three treatments produces a detectable PFS benefit in any plausible subgroup of this dataset, including their canonical mechanism-of-action subgroups (HER2+, ER+/postmeno, TNBC/HER2-low). All adjusted main effects are null or marginally negative; no subgroup analysis recovers a benefit.

analyses.code: Two-way joint subgroup scan over palbo modifiers + final 3-way regression
analyses.result_summary: Top 2-way joint subgroups for palbo: ER+/PIK3CA- diff=+2.38, PIK3CA-/PR+ +2.25, PIK3CA-/HER2-low +2.04, HER2-/PIK3CA- +2.04, ER+/HER2-low +1.94, ER+/HER2- +1.92, HER2-low/PR+ +1.84. The strongest signal is the conjunction of HR-positive (ER+/PR+/HER2-low favorable) AND HER2-negative AND PIK3CA-wild-type. Triple interaction model: ER+×HER2-×PIK3CA- captures essentially the entire palbo effect (subgroup-specific diff=+2.91 mo, outside diff=+0.003 mo).

analyses.code: joint subgroup scan over olaparib modifiers
analyses.result_summary: Top olaparib subgroups: BRCA2+/ER- +0.88 (p=0.007, n_tx=32); BRCA-any+/ER- +0.83 (p=0.0012, n_tx=55); BRCA+/TNBC +0.75 (p=0.022, n_tx=35); BRCA+/HER2+ +0.46 (p=0.25, n_tx=46); BRCA-any+ overall +0.35 (p=0.041, n_tx=239). BRCA-mutation+ER-negative is the cleanest olaparib-responsive subgroup; small n in BRCA+/ER+ limits inference but trend is attenuated.

analyses.code: Pembro x TNBC and stratified analyses
analyses.result_summary: Pembro effect: TNBC +0.072 (p=0.22, NS); non-TNBC -0.114 (p=0.0017); ER+ -0.111 (p=0.005); ER- +0.020 (p=0.67); HER2+/brain-mets -0.371 (p=0.031, n=151) — small n, exploratory. Interaction with TNBC p=0.015 confirmed; no positive subgroup reaches significance individually.

Trastuzumab: no subgroup shows positive effect (max in HER2+/non-stage-IV diff=-0.042). Tamoxifen: largest subgroup diff +0.109 (ER-/premeno, p=0.066, mechanistically implausible). Sacituzumab: largest subgroup diff +0.144 (ER-/HER2+, p=0.30, n_tx=279, mechanistically implausible). None of the three drugs generates a credible positive subgroup. Conclusion: in this dataset trastuzumab, tamoxifen, and sacituzumab are non-informative for pfs_months even in canonical indication subgroups.
